topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The discovery happened while I was tidying up Leo’s room, a task I usually approached with a calm, methodical air. Tucked deep within a rarely used secondary folder on his laptop, I found a login screen I didn't recognize. My heart gave a sudden, hard thump against my ribs. Curiosity warred with a deep, unsettling anxiety as I typed in the password I suspected he used. The screen lit up, revealing a feed filled with images and cryptic posts that felt alien and intensely private. When I clicked on a profile, the carefully curated facade of my son dissolved, replaced by a stranger engaging in activities I couldn't begin to comprehend. I quickly minimized the window, my hands starting to tremble slightly as I stared at the screen, a cold knot tightening in my stomach. What secrets was he keeping?

[story 2]

I walked into the kitchen, intending only to grab a glass of water, but the sight of the open browser window on Liam’s tablet stopped me dead in my tracks. It was a profile, meticulously maintained, full of posts that suggested a life entirely separate from the one we shared. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my breath catch in my throat. I leaned closer, my eyes scanning the barrage of unfamiliar usernames and shadowed pictures. Every scroll felt like an intrusion, a violation of a boundary I hadn't known existed. My palms grew damp, and I instinctively took a step back, the silence of the house suddenly amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. I needed to close it, erase it, but my fingers felt clumsy, locked in place by a paralyzing sense of alarm.

[story 3]

It was an accident, really. I was looking for a misplaced textbook and stumbled upon a notification banner on Maya’s phone—a notification I definitely hadn't authorized access to. The content was startling: a stream of short videos and candid photos that spoke of an entirely different social world. A sharp, cold sensation prickled the skin on my arms. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to flee the room, to physically remove myself from the proximity of that digital space. My voice came out as a strained whisper when I finally managed to navigate to the profile, the sheer volume of the activity making my vision swim slightly. I quickly pulled the phone away, my whole body rigid, waiting for something to happen, anything to confirm the unsettling reality I had just stumbled upon.
